title: Frozen 2 should be rated R
excerpt: Posted on Friday 17 Jul 2026. 1,014 words, 2 links. By Matt Webb.
content_html:
I watched Ferris Bueller’s Day Off which was as great as I remember.

It has an age rating of PG-13 in the US and 15 in the UK for bad language and some kissing. (There is also lying to parents but apparently that doesn’t count.)

This seems absurd.

I have way less problem with some light cursing/petting than films that are ostensibly for kids like cartoons and superhero movies that treat mass existential threat as an incident plot point in the hero’s journey.

Like:

  • Frozen 2 in which the entire city is almost destroyed by a tidal wave
  • the recent Superman movie in which the city actually is destroyed, and although it is reconstructed we never hear about the people crushed by debris etc.

Superman is rated PG-13 in the US, 12A in the UK. There’s off-screen loss of life but on-screen is widespread in movies: death with barely any emotional weight.

(a) I’m sure this lack of emotional weight in existential jeopardy is harmful to us.

(b) I’m sure that existential jeopardy in movies is getting more frequent; jeopardy inflation if you like.

(c) It is SO LAZY.

Look at Ferris Bueller: the jeopardy is a car with too many miles on the clock, and Ferris running to get home in time. My kid was on the edge of her seat.

But it takes effort and careful storytelling to set up jeopardy like this. We have to understand Cameron and his relationships and the car’s place in those, and we have to care about Cameron, and we have to feel the stakes, and the ominous arrival of the knowledge… If you were dropped into the movie 20 minutes from the end, you wouldn’t have that setup, it wouldn’t matter.

WHEREAS: death is death, its weight is universal.

So I can see how it flies through production meetings of hurried people. It’s an easy yes.

I am aware this smacks a bit of “oh films were different in my day, you didn’t need all this cursing and violence”…

So I checked.

(This is the joy of AI, you can cross-check your whims.)

I got ChatGPT to develop a jeopardy scoring methodology and here’s what it came up with, from low to high:

1 – Personal or social: embarrassment, exposure, rejection, career failure, losing a competition

2 – Family or bounded community: custody, home, livelihood, family separation, collapse of a small group

3 – Mortal but local: custody, home, livelihood, family separation, collapse of a small group

4 – Collective catastrophe: mass death; destruction or subjugation of a city, nation, population or people

5 – Existential catastrophe: extinction, civilisation, planet, universe, timeline or reality

So Ferris Bueller would be a “1” whereas Frozen 2 would be a “4.”

Then I got it to look up the top 10 films at the box office in north American each year for the last 50 years.

Then score all 500 films according to the main narrative jeopardy. (I assumed that knowledge would be in the training data seeing as these are popular movies.)

If you’re interested, [download the movie jeopardy spreadsheet here(/more/2026/07/film_jeopardy_1976_2025-by-chatgpt.xlsx)] (xlsx).

Finally I got ChatGPT to calculate the average per decade.

Here’s the results in the format:

Period: average jeopardy score (% low jeopardy movies – % high jeopardy movies)

  • 1976-79: 2.77 (43% low – 35% high)
  • 1980s: 2.83 (43% low – 31% high)
  • 1990s: 3.21 (28% – 46% high)
  • 2000s: 3.61 (24% low – 60% high)
  • 2010s: 3.96 (10% low – 72% high)
  • 2020-2025: 4.08 (7% low – 73% high)

i.e. I am not imagining this.

There has been considerable jeopardy inflation in movies between the 1980s and today.

So I think this is harmful to us all.

Death, as it is used in these movies, is not jeopardy but a symbol of jeopardy. That’s part of it being used lazily.

But we should put MASSIVE weight on death and calamity!

Because it’s a big deal! Especially violent death and collective danger.

And when we don’t give it weight, it gets assumed as part of the normal order of things. Which it is not.

One argument is: look Matt, these are cartoon deaths. It’s a superhero movie, it’s fantasy, it doesn’t matter, we know how to look past the lack of weight.

But all video are merely patterns of light on the screen; I don’t think we get to say that one matters more than another; they all occupy the exact same level of “remove” in our minds, and it all matters the same.

If something deserves weight when we see it on the TV news, it deserves weight when we see it in a movie.

Or else we carry the lack of weight when we see it in movies over to the TV news, and then it carries over to the real people depicted.

I always wonder what creates the consensus cosmogony for an era – our collective understanding of how the world works and in particular where we are going.

It was sci-fi in the 1950s (that’s the argument in the link above).

Now I would argue that we have identified at least a tributary of the 2020s consensus:

When we are all exposed to media that normalises death and mass threat, there is something in the psyche that kicks in…

When something built into your sense of reality isn’t happening, you work to make it happen.

Am I saying that we are deliberately, collectively creating situations of mass threat and emotionless jeopardy, because we see it in the movies and it isn’t happening in real life?

Yes. Unconsciously but yes.

So we need to find new collective futures, I’ve talked about that before. That’s a big lift.

But I would start by giving the events in movies the weight they deserve, so they don’t get normalised. We’d all be happier for it.

And that means I would start with the ratings systems.

Ferris Bueller, for all the cursing and kissing, is a movie for kids, give it a G.

Frozen 2 should be rated R.

(There is also lying to parents but apparently that doesn’t count.) This seems absurd. I have way less problem with some light cursing/petting than films that are ostensibly for kids like cartoons and superhero movies that treat mass existential threat as an incident plot point in the hero’s journey. Like: Frozen 2 in which the entire city is almost destroyed by a tidal wave the recent Superman movie in which the city actually is destroyed, and although it is reconstructed we never hear about the people crushed by debris etc. Superman is rated PG-13 in the US, 12A in the UK. There’s off-screen loss of life but on-screen is widespread in movies: death with barely any emotional weight. (a) I’m sure this lack of emotional weight in existential jeopardy is harmful to us. (b) I’m sure that existential jeopardy in movies is getting more frequent; jeopardy inflation if you like. (c) It is SO LAZY. Look at Ferris Bueller: the jeopardy is a car with too many miles on the clock, and Ferris running to get home in time. My kid was on the edge of her seat. But it takes effort and careful storytelling to set up jeopardy like this. We have to understand Cameron and his relationships and the car’s place in those, and we have to care about Cameron, and we have to feel the stakes, and the ominous arrival of the knowledge… If you were dropped into the movie 20 minutes from the end, you wouldn’t have that setup, it wouldn’t matter. WHEREAS: death is death, its weight is universal. So I can see how it flies through production meetings of hurried people. It’s an easy yes. I am aware this smacks a bit of “oh films were different in my day, you didn’t need all this cursing and violence”… So I checked. (This is the joy of AI, you can cross-check your whims.) I got ChatGPT to develop a jeopardy scoring methodology and here’s what it came up with, from low to high: 1 – Personal or social: embarrassment, exposure, rejection, career failure, losing a competition 2 – Family or bounded community: custody, home, livelihood, family separation, collapse of a small group 3 – Mortal but local: custody, home, livelihood, family separation, collapse of a small group 4 – Collective catastrophe: mass death; destruction or subjugation of a city, nation, population or people 5 – Existential catastrophe: extinction, civilisation, planet, universe, timeline or reality So Ferris Bueller would be a “1” whereas Frozen 2 would be a “4.” Then I got it to look up the top 10 films at the box office in north American each year for the last 50 years. Then score all 500 films according to the main narrative jeopardy. (I assumed that knowledge would be in the training data seeing as these are popular movies.) If you’re interested, [download the movie jeopardy spreadsheet here(/more/2026/07/film_jeopardy_1976_2025-by-chatgpt.xlsx)] (xlsx). Finally I got ChatGPT to calculate the average per decade. Here’s the results in the format: Period: average jeopardy score (% low jeopardy movies – % high jeopardy movies) 1976-79: 2.77 (43% low – 35% high) 1980s: 2.83 (43% low – 31% high) 1990s: 3.21 (28% – 46% high) 2000s: 3.61 (24% low – 60% high) 2010s: 3.96 (10% low – 72% high) 2020-2025: 4.08 (7% low – 73% high) i.e. I am not imagining this. There has been considerable jeopardy inflation in movies between the 1980s and today. So I think this is harmful to us all. Death, as it is used in these movies, is not jeopardy but a symbol of jeopardy. That’s part of it being used lazily. But we should put MASSIVE weight on death and calamity! Because it’s a big deal! Especially violent death and collective danger. And when we don’t give it weight, it gets assumed as part of the normal order of things. Which it is not. One argument is: look Matt, these are cartoon deaths. It’s a superhero movie, it’s fantasy, it doesn’t matter, we know how to look past the lack of weight. But all video are merely patterns of light on the screen; I don’t think we get to say that one matters more than another; they all occupy the exact same level of “remove” in our minds, and it all matters the same. If something deserves weight when we see it on the TV news, it deserves weight when we see it in a movie. Or else we carry the lack of weight when we see it in movies over to the TV news, and then it carries over to the real people depicted. I always wonder what creates the consensus cosmogony for an era – our collective understanding of how the world works and in particular where we are going. It was sci-fi in the 1950s (that’s the argument in the link above). Now I would argue that we have identified at least a tributary of the 2020s consensus: When we are all exposed to media that normalises death and mass threat, there is something in the psyche that kicks in… When something built into your sense of reality isn’t happening, you work to make it happen. Am I saying that we are deliberately, collectively creating situations of mass threat and emotionless jeopardy, because we see it in the movies and it isn’t happening in real life? Yes. Unconsciously but yes. So we need to find new collective futures, I’ve talked about that before. That’s a big lift. But I would start by giving the events in movies the weight they deserve, so they don’t get normalised. We’d all be happier for it. And that means I would start with the ratings systems. Ferris Bueller, for all the cursing and kissing, is a movie for kids, give it a G. Frozen 2 should be rated R.

blocks.html_en: I watched Ferris Bueller’s Day Off which was as great as I remember.

sentences.en: I watched Ferris Bueller’s Day Off which was as great as I remember.
sentences.zh: 我看了《费里斯·布勒的休息日》，那部电影和我记忆中一样精彩。

blocks.html_en: It has an age rating of PG-13 in the US and 15 in the UK for bad language and some kissing. (There is also lying to parents but apparently that doesn’t count.)

sentences.en: It has an age rating of PG-13 in the US and 15 in the UK for bad language and some kissing.
sentences.zh: 由于脏话和接吻，该剧在美国的年龄评级为 PG-13，在英国的年龄评级为 15。

sentences.en: (There is also lying to parents but apparently that doesn’t count.)
sentences.zh: （也有对父母撒谎的情况，但显然这不算数。）

blocks.html_en: This seems absurd.

sentences.en: This seems absurd.
sentences.zh: 这似乎很荒谬。

blocks.html_en: I have way less problem with some light cursing/petting than films that are ostensibly for kids like cartoons and superhero movies that treat mass existential threat as an incident plot point in the hero’s journey.

sentences.en: I have way less problem with some light cursing/petting than films that are ostensibly for kids like cartoons and superhero movies that treat mass existential threat as an incident plot point in the hero’s journey.
sentences.zh: 与那些表面上适合孩子的电影（例如卡通片和超级英雄电影）相比，我对一些轻微的咒骂/爱抚的问题要少得多，这些电影将大规模的生存威胁视为英雄旅程中的一个事件情节点。

blocks.html_en: Like:
• Frozen 2 in which the entire city is almost destroyed by a tidal wave

sentences.en: Frozen 2 in which the entire city is almost destroyed by a tidal wave
sentences.zh: 《冰雪奇缘2》中，整个城市几乎被海啸摧毁

blocks.html_en: • the recent Superman movie in which the city actually is destroyed, and although it is reconstructed we never hear about the people crushed by debris etc.

sentences.en: the recent Superman movie in which the city actually is destroyed, and although it is reconstructed we never hear about the people crushed by debris etc.
sentences.zh: 最近的超人电影中，这座城市实际上被摧毁了，尽管它被重建了，但我们从未听说过人们被碎片压碎等。

blocks.html_en: Superman is rated PG-13 in the US, 12A in the UK. There’s off-screen loss of life but on-screen is widespread in movies: death with barely any emotional weight.

sentences.en: Superman is rated PG-13 in the US, 12A in the UK.
sentences.zh: 《超人》在美国的评级为 PG-13，在英国的评级为 12A。

sentences.en: There’s off-screen loss of life but on-screen is widespread in movies: death with barely any emotional weight.
sentences.zh: 银幕外的生命损失，但银幕上的生命损失在电影中很常见：几乎没有任何情感负担的死亡。

blocks.html_en: (a) I’m sure this lack of emotional weight in existential jeopardy is harmful to us.

sentences.en: (a) I’m sure this lack of emotional weight in existential jeopardy is harmful to us.
sentences.zh: (a) 我确信在生存危机中缺乏情感分量对我们有害。

blocks.html_en: (b) I’m sure that existential jeopardy in movies is getting more frequent; jeopardy inflation if you like.

sentences.en: (b) I’m sure that existential jeopardy in movies is getting more frequent; jeopardy inflation if you like.
sentences.zh: (b) 我确信电影中的生存危机越来越频繁；如果你愿意的话，可以说是通货膨胀的危险。

blocks.html_en: (c) It is SO LAZY.

sentences.en: (c) It is SO LAZY.
sentences.zh: (c) 太懒了。

blocks.html_en: Look at Ferris Bueller: the jeopardy is a car with too many miles on the clock, and Ferris running to get home in time. My kid was on the edge of her seat.

sentences.en: Look at Ferris Bueller: the jeopardy is a car with too many miles on the clock, and Ferris running to get home in time.
sentences.zh: 看看费里斯·布勒（Ferris Bueller）：危险在于一辆汽车的里程数太多，而费里斯却要及时赶回家。

sentences.en: My kid was on the edge of her seat.
sentences.zh: 我的孩子正坐在座位边上。

blocks.html_en: But it takes effort and careful storytelling to set up jeopardy like this. We have to understand Cameron and his relationships and the car’s place in those, and we have to care about Cameron, and we have to feel the stakes, and the ominous arrival of the knowledge… If you were dropped into the movie 20 minutes from the end, you wouldn’t have that setup, it wouldn’t matter.

sentences.en: But it takes effort and careful storytelling to set up jeopardy like this.
sentences.zh: 但要设置这样的危险需要努力和仔细的讲故事。

sentences.en: We have to understand Cameron and his relationships and the car’s place in those, and we have to care about Cameron, and we have to feel the stakes, and the ominous arrival of the knowledge…
sentences.zh: 我们必须了解卡梅伦和他的关系以及汽车在其中的地位，我们必须关心卡梅伦，我们必须感受到风险，以及知识的不祥到来......

sentences.en: If you were dropped into the movie 20 minutes from the end, you wouldn’t have that setup, it wouldn’t matter.
sentences.zh: 如果你在电影结束后 20 分钟就被扔进电影里，你就没有这样的设置，那也没关系。

blocks.html_en: WHEREAS: death is death, its weight is universal.

sentences.en: WHEREAS: death is death, its weight is universal.
sentences.zh: 鉴于：死亡就是死亡，它的重量是普遍的。

blocks.html_en: So I can see how it flies through production meetings of hurried people. It’s an easy yes.

sentences.en: So I can see how it flies through production meetings of hurried people.
sentences.zh: 所以我可以看到它是如何在匆忙的人们的生产会议中飞驰的。

sentences.en: It’s an easy yes.
sentences.zh: 这很简单。

blocks.html_en: I am aware this smacks a bit of “oh films were different in my day, you didn’t need all this cursing and violence”…

sentences.en: I am aware this smacks a bit of “oh films were different in my day, you didn’t need all this cursing and violence”…
sentences.zh: 我知道这有点“哦，我那个时代的电影不一样，你不需要所有这些咒骂和暴力”......

blocks.html_en: So I checked.

sentences.en: So I checked.
sentences.zh: 所以我检查了。

blocks.html_en: (This is the joy of AI, you can cross-check your whims.)

sentences.en: (This is the joy of AI, you can cross-check your whims.)
sentences.zh: （这就是AI的乐趣，你可以反复检查你的奇思妙想。）

blocks.html_en: I got ChatGPT to develop a jeopardy scoring methodology and here’s what it came up with, from low to high:

sentences.en: I got ChatGPT to develop a jeopardy scoring methodology and here’s what it came up with, from low to high:
sentences.zh: 我让 ChatGPT 开发了一种危险评分方法，以下是它的评分方法，从低到高：

blocks.html_en:
1 – Personal or social: embarrassment, exposure, rejection, career failure, losing a competition

2 – Family or bounded community: custody, home, livelihood, family separation, collapse of a small group

3 – Mortal but local: custody, home, livelihood, family separation, collapse of a small group

4 – Collective catastrophe: mass death; destruction or subjugation of a city, nation, population or people

5 – Existential catastrophe: extinction, civilisation, planet, universe, timeline or reality

sentences.zh: 1 – 个人或社会：尴尬、暴露、拒绝、职业失败、输掉比赛 2 – 家庭或有界社区：监护权、家庭、生计、家庭分离、小群体崩溃 3 – 凡人但本地：监护权、家庭、生计、家庭分离、小群体崩溃 4 – 集体灾难：大规模死亡；城市、国家、人口或民族的毁灭或征服 5 – 存在性灾难：灭绝、文明、行星、宇宙、时间线或现实

sentences.en: So I think this is harmful to us all.
sentences.zh: 所以我认为这对我们所有人都有害。

blocks.html_en: Death, as it is used in these movies, is not jeopardy but a symbol of jeopardy. That’s part of it being used lazily.

sentences.en: Death, as it is used in these movies, is not jeopardy but a symbol of jeopardy.
sentences.zh: 这些电影中使用的死亡并不是危险，而是危险的象征。

sentences.en: That’s part of it being used lazily.
sentences.zh: 这是它被懒惰使用的一部分。

blocks.html_en: But we should put MASSIVE weight on death and calamity!

sentences.en: But we should put MASSIVE weight on death and calamity!
sentences.zh: 但我们应该高度重视死亡和灾难！

blocks.html_en: Because it’s a big deal! Especially violent death and collective danger.

sentences.zh: 因为这是一件大事！

sentences.zh: 尤其是暴力死亡和集体危险。

blocks.html_en: And when we don’t give it weight, it gets assumed as part of the normal order of things. Which it is not.

sentences.zh: 当我们不重视它时，它就会被视为事物正常秩序的一部分。

sentences.zh: 但事实并非如此。

blocks.html_en: One argument is: look Matt, these are cartoon deaths. It’s a superhero movie, it’s fantasy, it doesn’t matter, we know how to look past the lack of weight.

sentences.en: One argument is: look Matt, these are cartoon deaths.
sentences.zh: 一种说法是：看马特，这些都是卡通死亡。

sentences.en: It’s a superhero movie, it’s fantasy, it doesn’t matter, we know how to look past the lack of weight.
sentences.zh: 这是一部超级英雄电影，是奇幻电影，没关系，我们知道如何克服体重不足的问题。

blocks.html_en: But all video are merely patterns of light on the screen; I don’t think we get to say that one matters more than another; they all occupy the exact same level of “remove” in our minds, and it all matters the same.

sentences.en: But all video are merely patterns of light on the screen; I don’t think we get to say that one matters more than another; they all occupy the exact same level of “remove” in our minds, and it all matters the same.
sentences.zh: 但所有视频都只是屏幕上的光图案；我认为我们不能说一个比另一个更重要；它们在我们的脑海中都占据着完全相同的“删除”级别，而且这一切都是一样的。

blocks.html_en: If something deserves weight when we see it on the TV news, it deserves weight when we see it in a movie.

sentences.en: If something deserves weight when we see it on the TV news, it deserves weight when we see it in a movie.
sentences.zh: 如果我们在电视新闻中看到某件事值得重视，那么当我们在电影中看到它时它也值得重视。

blocks.html_en: Or else we carry the lack of weight when we see it in movies over to the TV news, and then it carries over to the real people depicted.

sentences.en: Or else we carry the lack of weight when we see it in movies over to the TV news, and then it carries over to the real people depicted.
sentences.zh: 否则，当我们在电影中看到电视新闻时，我们就会把这种缺乏分量的感觉带到电视新闻中，然后它又会延续到所描绘的真实人物身上。

blocks.html_en: I always wonder what creates the consensus cosmogony for an era – our collective understanding of how the world works and in particular where we are going.

sentences.en: I always wonder what creates the consensus cosmogony for an era – our collective understanding of how the world works and in particular where we are going.
sentences.zh: 我总是想知道是什么创造了一个时代的宇宙观共识——我们对世界如何运作，特别是我们将走向何方的集体理解。

blocks.html_en: It was sci-fi in the 1950s (that’s the argument in the link above).

sentences.en: It was sci-fi in the 1950s (that’s the argument in the link above).
sentences.zh: 这是 20 世纪 50 年代的科幻小说（这是上面链接中的论点）。

blocks.html_en: Now I would argue that we have identified at least a tributary of the 2020s consensus:

sentences.en: Now I would argue that we have identified at least a tributary of the 2020s consensus:
sentences.zh: 现在我认为我们至少已经确定了 2020 年代共识的一个支流：

blocks.html_en: When we are all exposed to media that normalises death and mass threat, there is something in the psyche that kicks in…

sentences.en: When we are all exposed to media that normalises death and mass threat, there is something in the psyche that kicks in…
sentences.zh: 当我们都接触到将死亡和大规模威胁常态化的媒体时，心灵中就会有一些东西开始发挥作用……

blocks.html_en: When something built into your sense of reality isn’t happening, you work to make it happen.

sentences.en: When something built into your sense of reality isn’t happening, you work to make it happen.
sentences.zh: 当你现实感中的某些事情没有发生时，你就会努力让它发生。

blocks.html_en: Am I saying that we are deliberately, collectively creating situations of mass threat and emotionless jeopardy, because we see it in the movies and it isn’t happening in real life?

sentences.en: Am I saying that we are deliberately, collectively creating situations of mass threat and emotionless jeopardy, because we see it in the movies and it isn’t happening in real life?
sentences.zh: 我是不是在说，我们正在故意集体制造大规模威胁和毫无感情的危险，因为我们在电影中看到了这种情况，而现实生活中却没有发生？

blocks.html_en: Yes. Unconsciously but yes.

sentences.en: Yes.
sentences.zh: 是的。

sentences.en: Unconsciously but yes.
sentences.zh: 不知不觉却是的。

blocks.html_en: So we need to find new collective futures, I’ve talked about that before. That’s a big lift.

sentences.en: So we need to find new collective futures, I’ve talked about that before.
sentences.zh: 所以我们需要找到新的集体未来，我之前已经谈到过。

sentences.en: That’s a big lift.
sentences.zh: 这是一个很大的提升。

blocks.html_en: But I would start by giving the events in movies the weight they deserve, so they don’t get normalised. We’d all be happier for it.

sentences.en: But I would start by giving the events in movies the weight they deserve, so they don’t get normalised.
sentences.zh: 但我首先要给予电影中的事件应有的重视，这样它们就不会被正常化。

sentences.en: We’d all be happier for it.
sentences.zh: 我们都会为此感到高兴。

blocks.html_en: And that means I would start with the ratings systems.

sentences.en: And that means I would start with the ratings systems.
sentences.zh: 这意味着我将从评级系统开始。

blocks.html_en: Ferris Bueller, for all the cursing and kissing, is a movie for kids, give it a G.

sentences.en: Ferris Bueller, for all the cursing and kissing, is a movie for kids, give it a G.
sentences.zh: 《费里斯·布勒》虽然充满了咒骂和亲吻，但它是一部适合儿童的电影，给它一个G。

blocks.html_en: Frozen 2 should be rated R.

sentences.en: Frozen 2 should be rated R.
sentences.zh: 《冰雪奇缘2》应该被评为R级。

content_html_zh:
我看了《费里斯·布勒的休息日》，那部电影和我记忆中一样精彩。

由于脏话和接吻，该剧在美国的年龄评级为 PG-13，在英国的年龄评级为 15。 （也有对父母撒谎的情况，但显然这不算数。）

这似乎很荒谬。

与那些表面上适合孩子的电影（例如卡通片和超级英雄电影）相比，我对一些轻微的咒骂/爱抚的问题要少得多，这些电影将大规模的生存威胁视为英雄旅程中的一个事件情节点。

Like:

  • 《冰雪奇缘2》中，整个城市几乎被海啸摧毁
  • 最近的超人电影中，这座城市实际上被摧毁了，尽管它被重建了，但我们从未听说过人们被碎片压碎等。

《超人》在美国的评级为 PG-13，在英国的评级为 12A。 银幕外的生命损失，但银幕上的生命损失在电影中很常见：几乎没有任何情感负担的死亡。

(a) 我确信在生存危机中缺乏情感分量对我们有害。

(b) 我确信电影中的生存危机越来越频繁；如果你愿意的话，可以说是通货膨胀的危险。

(c) 太懒了。

看看费里斯·布勒（Ferris Bueller）：危险在于一辆汽车的里程数太多，而费里斯却要及时赶回家。 我的孩子正坐在座位边上。

但要设置这样的危险需要努力和仔细的讲故事。 我们必须了解卡梅伦和他的关系以及汽车在其中的地位，我们必须关心卡梅伦，我们必须感受到风险，以及知识的不祥到来...... 如果你在电影结束后 20 分钟就被扔进电影里，你就没有这样的设置，那也没关系。

鉴于：死亡就是死亡，它的重量是普遍的。

所以我可以看到它是如何在匆忙的人们的生产会议中飞驰的。 这很简单。

我知道这有点“哦，我那个时代的电影不一样，你不需要所有这些咒骂和暴力”......

所以我检查了。

（这就是AI的乐趣，你可以反复检查你的奇思妙想。）

我让 ChatGPT 开发了一种危险评分方法，以下是它的评分方法，从低到高：

1 – 个人或社会：尴尬、暴露、拒绝、职业失败、输掉比赛 2 – 家庭或有界社区：监护权、家庭、生计、家庭分离、小群体崩溃 3 – 凡人但本地：监护权、家庭、生计、家庭分离、小群体崩溃 4 – 集体灾难：大规模死亡；城市、国家、人口或民族的毁灭或征服 5 – 存在性灾难：灭绝、文明、行星、宇宙、时间线或现实

因此，Ferris Bueller 将是“1”，而《冰雪奇缘 2》将是“4”。

然后我用它来查找过去50年每年北美票房前10名的电影。

然后根据主要叙事危险对所有 500 部电影进行评分。 （我认为知识将存在于训练数据中，因为这些都是流行电影。）

如果您有兴趣，[在此处下载电影危险电子表格(/more/2026/07/film_jeopardy_1976_2025-by-chatgpt.xlsx)] (xlsx)。

最后我用 ChatGPT 来计算每十年的平均值。

以下是格式的结果：

期间：平均危险分数（低危险电影百分比 – 高危险电影百分比）

  • 1976-79: 2.77 (43% low – 35% high)
  • 1980 年代：2.83（低点 43% – 高点 31%）
  • 1990s: 3.21 (28% – 46% high)
  • 2000 年代：3.61（低 24% – 高 60%）
  • 2010 年代：3.96（低点 10% – 高点 72%）
  • 2020-2025: 4.08 (7% low – 73% high)

也就是说，我不是在想象这一点。

从 20 世纪 80 年代至今，电影中的危险膨胀现象相当严重。

所以我认为这对我们所有人都有害。

这些电影中使用的死亡并不是危险，而是危险的象征。 这是它被懒惰使用的一部分。

但我们应该高度重视死亡和灾难！

因为这是一件大事！ 尤其是暴力死亡和集体危险。

当我们不重视它时，它就会被视为事物正常秩序的一部分。 但事实并非如此。

一种说法是：看马特，这些都是卡通死亡。 这是一部超级英雄电影，是奇幻电影，没关系，我们知道如何克服体重不足的问题。

但所有视频都只是屏幕上的光图案；我认为我们不能说一个比另一个更重要；它们在我们的脑海中都占据着完全相同的“删除”级别，而且这一切都是一样的。

如果我们在电视新闻中看到某件事值得重视，那么当我们在电影中看到它时它也值得重视。

否则，当我们在电影中看到电视新闻时，我们就会把这种缺乏分量的感觉带到电视新闻中，然后它又会延续到所描绘的真实人物身上。

我总是想知道是什么创造了一个时代的宇宙观共识——我们对世界如何运作，特别是我们将走向何方的集体理解。

这是 20 世纪 50 年代的科幻小说（这是上面链接中的论点）。

现在我认为我们至少已经确定了 2020 年代共识的一个支流：

当我们都接触到将死亡和大规模威胁常态化的媒体时，心灵中就会有一些东西开始发挥作用……

当你现实感中的某些事情没有发生时，你就会努力让它发生。

我是不是在说，我们正在故意集体制造大规模威胁和毫无感情的危险，因为我们在电影中看到了这种情况，而现实生活中却没有发生？

是的。 不知不觉却是的。

所以我们需要找到新的集体未来，我之前已经谈到过。 这是一个很大的提升。

但我首先要给予电影中的事件应有的重视，这样它们就不会被正常化。 我们都会为此感到高兴。

这意味着我将从评级系统开始。

《费里斯·布勒》虽然充满了咒骂和亲吻，但它是一部适合儿童的电影，给它一个G。

《冰雪奇缘2》应该被评为R级。
